C.fingering: Index on B1, middle on D2, ring on A3. Let high E ring.
C.transition: Pivot your ring finger when moving between C and Am/F.
C.commonMistake: Mute the high E string; keep it open.

G.fingering: Ring on low E3, middle on A2, pinky on high E3.
G.transition: Keep ring/pinky planted when moving to D or Em.
G.commonMistake: Letting the B string mute; curl your fingers.

D.fingering: Index on G2, ring on B3, middle on high E2.
D.transition: Make a triangle shape and lift together for quick changes.
D.commonMistake: Low strings ringing; mute low E/A.

Em.fingering: Middle on A2, ring on D2.
Em.transition: Slide from G by dropping the middle finger first.
Em.commonMistake: Pressing too hard; keep it relaxed.

Am.fingering: Index on B1, middle on D2, ring on G2.
Am.transition: From C, keep the index on B1 and pivot.
Am.commonMistake: B string muted; keep knuckles arched.

F.fingering: Barre at 1st fret, ring on A3, pinky on D3, middle on G2.
F.transition: Practice mini-barre on B/E first, then add bass.
F.commonMistake: Buzzing on high strings; roll index slightly.

E.fingering: Index on G1, middle on A2, ring on D2.
E.transition: Move as a block from Em (add index).
E.commonMistake: Muted G string; keep index arched.

A.fingering: Index on D2, middle on G2, ring on B2.
A.transition: Keep fingers compact to avoid muting high E.
A.commonMistake: High E muted; angle fingers toward the nut.
